---
description: Dispatch code-reviewer and integration-reviewer on staged changes in parallel; reconcile verdicts
model: opus
---
> Cursor-side mirror of `/review`. Source of truth: `.claude/skills/review/SKILL.md`.


# /review

Run both the code and integration reviewers against the same scope in parallel, then reconcile their verdicts.

## When to run

- Before every non-trivial commit (mandatory per the review pipeline in `.claude/agents/README.md`)
- Before pushing a feature branch
- When `/verify` surfaces review-level findings

## Procedure

1. **Determine the scope.**
   - If `range` argument supplied, use it
   - Else if anything staged, use `git diff --cached`
   - Else use `HEAD~1..HEAD`
   - Else error — nothing to review
2. **Dispatch in parallel** (two agents, single message with two Agent tool calls):
   - `code-reviewer` with the scope
   - `integration-reviewer` with the scope
3. **Reconcile verdicts.**

   | code-reviewer | integration-reviewer | Combined           |
   | ------------- | -------------------- | ------------------ |
   | APPROVE       | SAFE                 | COMMIT-READY       |
   | APPROVE       | RISKY                | PROCEED WITH NOTES |
   | APPROVE       | BREAKING             | FIX REQUIRED       |
   | WARNING       | SAFE                 | PROCEED WITH NOTES |
   | WARNING       | RISKY                | PROCEED WITH NOTES |
   | WARNING       | BREAKING             | FIX REQUIRED       |
   | BLOCK         | any                  | FIX REQUIRED       |
   | any           | BREAKING             | FIX REQUIRED       |

4. **If FIX REQUIRED**: the main agent is expected to iterate (per the auto-re-review loop, up to 3 iterations). Record iteration count in the output.
5. **Summarize for the caller.**

## Output shape

```markdown
# /review result — <scope>

## Verdicts

- **code-reviewer**: APPROVE | WARNING | BLOCK
  <one-line summary>
- **integration-reviewer**: SAFE | RISKY | BREAKING
  <one-line summary>

## Combined: COMMIT-READY | PROCEED WITH NOTES | FIX REQUIRED

## Findings (grouped)

### Critical / Breaking (N)

- <findings that require a fix>

### High / Risky (N)

- <findings that warrant attention>

### Medium / Low (N)

- <findings that are notes, not blockers>

## Iteration

- Round 1 / N (cap 3 per ADR #0001)
- Carry-forward fixes from prior rounds: <list>

## Next step

- If COMMIT-READY: commit
- If PROCEED WITH NOTES: acknowledge each note; commit
- If FIX REQUIRED: list the fixes the implementer should apply, then
  re-run `/review`. If after 3 iterations still blocked, file an ADR
  under `.claude/decisions/` appealing the relevant rule
```

## What /review does NOT do

- Run the security reviewer — that's `/security-review`, a separate skill with its own verdict vocabulary
- Run the test suite — that's `/verify`
- Auto-fix findings — it reports; the implementer fixes

## Failure mode

If no scope is resolvable (no stage, no commits), return an error-style response with a suggestion to stage or pass an explicit range.

## Time / cost

Opus-tier. Both agents run in parallel on the same input, each time-boxed at 10 minutes.
